data.title: As an agent, I can consult a shared prior-art search framework before writing code
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create the shared prior-art search prompt fragment — the core decision framework that agents consult before writing or modifying code.
**Why**: Agents currently exhibit confident ignorance when encountering unfamiliar tools or patterns, wasting cycles on broken solutions instead of searching for proven implementations.
**Scope**:
- IN: Bright-line trigger checklist, trust validation gate (bugs=hard blocker, CI/git=blockers, tests/usage=soft signals), tiered search protocol (project docs → narrow GitHub ~6 calls → broad outcome ~10 calls → user escalation), routine modification exclusions, non-interactive fallback (structured result instead of blocking), relationship to fix-bug's existing Empirical Validation Directive
- OUT: Integration into consuming skills (Story 2), eval suite (Story 3), changes to existing fix-bug investigation agents

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a shared prompt fragment at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/ defines bright-line triggers as an enumerable checklist for prior-art search decisions
  ← Satisfies: "A shared prompt fragment defines bright-line triggers as an enumerable checklist"
- When this story is complete, the fragment defines a trust validation gate with explicit decision rule: hard blockers (open bugs, CI failures) evaluated first and cause untrusted regardless of soft signals; soft signals (tests, usage) only apply when no hard blockers present
  ← Satisfies: "Agents check project documentation first, with a trust validation gate"
- When this story is complete, the fragment defines tiered search: narrow GitHub (~6 calls) → broad outcome-reframed (~10 calls) → user escalation, with structured non-interactive fallback
  ← Satisfies: "Agents execute a narrow GitHub code search, then broader search, then escalate"
- When this story is complete, the triggers explicitly exclude routine modifications: single-file logic changes within existing patterns, test reversions, formatting/lint fixes
  ← Satisfies: "Bright-line triggers explicitly exclude routine modifications"
- When this story is complete, the fragment includes a section clarifying its relationship to fix-bug's existing Empirical Validation Directive (lines 39-52) — either superseding it or declaring which concerns each addresses
  ← Satisfies: cross-story consistency (adversarial review finding)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Trust validation gate references ticket CLI and CI status — these must remain stable interfaces
